headline: Hank Hill Returns: 'King of the Hill' Revival Drops Full Season on Hulu
lede: Propane enthusiasts and animated comedy fans, rejoice. Hulu is reigniting the beloved animated series with a surprise full-season drop this July.
body:
Fans of Arlen, Texas' most sardonic suburban patriarch can finally mark their calendars. Hulu will resurrect Mike Judge's beloved animated series *King of the Hill* with a complete 10-episode seasonal run, delivering the entire narrative arc in one streaming package.

The revival signals a significant streaming strategy shift, allowing viewers to consume the entire narrative arc at their own pace. Judge's returning voice cast promises to recapture the nuanced humor that made the original series a cult classic among animated comedy devotees.

The series, which originally ran from 1997 to 2010, follows propane salesman Hank Hill, his family, and eccentric neighborhood friends through suburban Texas life. Its understated comedic approach and sympathetic portrayal of working-class American dynamics set it apart from more frenetic animated comedies of its era.

While plot details remain closely guarded, early indicators suggest the new season will maintain the show's signature blend of gentle satire and genuine character development. Longtime fans can expect the same meticulous attention to cultural minutiae that defined the original run.
